chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH, CIRCUIT COURT HYDERABAD Criminal Bail Application No.S- 148 of 2025 DATE ORDER WITH SIGNATURE OF JUDGE(S) 1. For orders on M.A.No. 1024/2025. (urgent application) 2. For orders on office objections. 3. For orders on M.A.No.1025 2025. (exemption Application) 4. For hearing of main case. 12.02.2025 Mr. Inam Ali Malik Advocate for the Applicant along with Applicants. == 1. Urgency is granted. 2. Overruled. 3. Exemption application is allowed subject to all exceptions. 4. Through instant Criminal Bail Application, Applicants Ghayasuddin Ahmed and Muhammad Bilal seek protective bail in FIR No.03/2024 registered at PS FIA Composite Circle Shaheed Benazirabad for offences under sections 409, 420, 468, 471, 477-A, 201, 109 of PPS r/w 5 of PCA, 1947. Learned Counsel states that the Applicants were not named in the FIR but at later stage in the challan they have been added as accused and expressing apprehension of unjust and malafide arrest requests for protective bail. He also referred to page 319 which according to him is case dairy dated 10.02.2025 in the subject FIR No.03/2024 of PS FIA Composite Circle Shaheed Benazirabad and states that while accepting the challan learned Trial Court has also issued NBWs against the present Applicants Ghayasuddin Ahmed and Muhammad Bilal who are shown as absconders. He requests for suspension of the NBWs in order to allow the Applicants to appear before the Trial Court. Be that as it may, without touching merits of the case, applicant is admitted to protective bail for a period of seven days from today and NBWs are suspended subject to furnishing his solvent surety in the sum of Rs.30,000/- each and P.R bond in like amount to the satisfaction of

He requests for suspension of the NBWs in order to allow the Applicants to appear before the Trial Court. Be that as it may, without touching merits of the case, applicant is admitted to protective bail for a period of seven days from today and NBWs are suspended subject to furnishing his solvent surety in the sum of Rs.30,000/- each and P.R bond in like amount to the satisfaction of Additional Registrar of this Court and further proceedings to be conducted on merits in accordance with law. This order shall cease to have its effect on expiry of seven days from today or on the date the applicants surrender before the concerned Court whichever occurs earlier; in case of failure, the bail bond executed by the applicants shall stand forfeited automatically. Criminal Bail Application stands disposed of in above terms. JUDGE
